channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Democrats fear chances of bringing home mistakenly deported man slipping away
item.description: Congressional Democrats are growing concerned that their hopes of bringing home a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a high-security prison in El Salvador may be slipping away, even as they struggle with how to push for his return.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Sudanese paramilitary group says its forming a rival government
item.description: A notorious paramilitary group fighting against the Sudanese military says its forming a rival government, which will rule parts of the country controlled by the group including the western Darfur region.
item.dc:creator: By Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli defense minister says troops will remain in Gaza, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely
item.description: Israel’s defense minister says that troops will remain in so-called security zones in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and Syria indefinitely.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In the Ukrainian city of Sumy, life goes on despite the constant threat of attack
item.description: SUMY, Ukraine (AP) — The humdrum of daily life in Sumy belies the constant threat of death its people have lived with since Russia invaded Ukraine three years ago.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: The first lawsuit is filed over the deadly nightclub roof collapse in the Dominican Republic
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — The first known lawsuit has been filed against the owners of a legendary nightclub whose roof collapsed and killed 231 people in the Dominican Republic.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mozambique carried out a brutal crackdown on post-election protests, Amnesty says
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — Mozambique's security forces carried out a brutal, three-month crackdown on protesters after the country's election last year, a leading international rights group said Wednesday, citing local activists who alleged that more than 300 people were killed and more than 3,
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY and CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: France expels 12 Algerian officials in tit-for-tat move amid diplomatic tensions
item.description: PARIS (AP) — France said Tuesday it was expelling 12 Algerian diplomatic officials a day after Algeria announced the expulsion of the same number of French officials in escalating tensions between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US blasts UN for extending human rights envoy to Palestinian territories
item.description: The United States is rebuking a United Nations decision to extend Francesca Albanese’s tenure as the U.N. special rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian territories.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Hamas rejects Israeli ceasefire, hostage return deal over disarmament demands
item.description: Hamas has reportedly rejected Israel's latest ceasefire offer, which included the return of humanitarian aid in exchange for nearly a dozen hostages and Hamas' complete disarmament.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Another US aircraft carrier in Mideast waters ahead of second round of Iran-US nuclear talks
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A second U.S. aircraft carrier is operating in Mideast waters ahead of the next round of talks between Iran and the United States over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program, satellite photos analyzed Tuesday by The Associated Press showed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: A Russian court has convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chinese Embassy accuses Bessent of 'maliciously slandering' Beijing
item.description: The Chinese Embassy in Argentina has criticized U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent for “maliciously slandering” the country as it continues to fund development assistance for African and Latin American countries. “We advise the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Singapore dissolves parliament, paving the way for May 3 general election
item.description: Singapore’s parliament was dissolved Tuesday, paving the way for general elections in which the city-state’s long-ruling People’s Action Party will seek to strengthen its dominance under Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas says it's sending a delegation to Qatar to continue Gaza ceasefire talks
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Hamas official said Monday that the Palestinian militant group is sending a delegation to the Gulf state of Qatar to continue indirect ceasefire talks with Israel over the war in Gaza, as the territory’s Health Ministry said that 38 people were confirmed dead over
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Haiti's government adopts a 'war budget' as town after town falls to gangs
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Haiti’s government announced Monday that it approved what it called a “war budget” of $275,000 aimed at alleviating the country’s crisis as gang violence surges.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New York City's mayor visits the Dominican Republic after the deadly nightclub roof collapse
item.description: SANTO DOMINGO, Dominican Republic (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams arrived in the Dominican Republic on Monday to pay his respects to the scores of people who died last week when the roof of a popular nightclub collapsed in the country's capital, Santo Domingo.
item.dc:creator: MARTÍN ADAMES ALCÁNTARA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Iran must ditch ‘concept of a nuclear weapon’ ahead of more talks
item.description: President Donald Trump says Iran "wants to deal" with Washington, but he once again demanded that the world's number one terror sponsor drop any "concept of a nuclear weapon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspected US airstrikes kill at least 7 people in Yemen, Houthi rebels say
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes around Yemen's rebel-held capital killed at least seven people and wounded 29 overnight, the Houthis said Monday as they also claimed shooting down another American MQ-9 Reaper drone.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trade war with China hurting US farmers, advocate says
item.description: John Boyd Jr., a fourth-generation family farmer who works 1,300 acres in Virginia, said he hopes the Trump administration will listen to farmers moving forward.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump admin secures release of American missionary held in Tunisia for 13 months: report
item.description: American missionary Robert Vieira, detained in Tunisia for more than a year, is set to return home soon after officials secured his release on Sunday, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 Ukrainians killed by Russian ballistics in Palm Sunday strike
item.description: Russia hit the Ukrainian city of Sumy with two missiles as civilian celebrated Palm Sunday, killing more than 30 people and injuring dozens of others, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin makes new move to bring South African refugees to US as president blasts nation's rulers again
item.description: As the U.S. begins refugee processing for South African Afrikaners, a group is pushing Washington to support its bid for an autonomous Afrikaner homeland within South Africa.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Woman unknowingly gives birth to a stranger's baby after IVF clinic error
item.description: A woman in Australia unknowingly gave birth to a stranger’s baby after she received another patient's embryo from her in vitro fertilization clinic due to “human error,” the clinic said.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: China ramps up military ‘rehearsals’ around Taiwan, outstrips US in air, maritime, space
item.description: Top Indo-Pacific commander warned senators that China's aggression around Taiwan is not an "exercise" but a "rehearsal" for when it finally decides to "reunify" the island with the mainland.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Exclusive: Prince Harry, King Charles' rift widens; Charles refused to see Harry in UK
item.description: Prince Harry also raised eyebrows this weekend when he missed the wedding of close friend, Lord Vivian.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Countries will follow Trump imposing tariffs on China: Gordon Chang
item.description: Chang, author of the new book "Plan Red: China's Project to Destroy America," said he expects to see tariffs rise on Chinese goods worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation
